nodes.title: Movement & Improvisation
nodes.description: Guided exploration in dance elements for the creative development of personal movement vocabulary, spontaneous group interaction, as well as overall individual and environmental awareness.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to degree and PACE students. Students must be fundamentally interested in wider concepts of movement improvisation, performance, and collaboration and remain active in their inquiry throughout the semester.

nodes.title: Contemporary: Advanced
nodes.description: Advanced level contemporary dance technique. Focus on advanced skills for performance, conditioning, and exploration of digital performance. Reading, writing, and attending live performances required. May be repeated for credit. Prerequisite: DNCE 2100 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Reading, writing, and attending live performances required.

nodes.title: African Forms
nodes.description: A detailed study of the practice, history, and cultural significance of African and/or African-derived dance forms. Major emphasis on physical training.

nodes.title: Site Performance Practicum
nodes.description: Participation in faculty-supervised site-based performances. Emphasis on creative research that leads to performance. Includes focus on performance development/rehearsal, music accompaniment/composition, and/or technical/design preparation leading to a fully realized public performance. Prerequisite: Audition or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Audition or Instructor permission. PACE students with permission and override. Willingness to create movement and collaborate creatively. Willingness to dance outside (may get dirty!)

nodes.title: Student Dance Project
nodes.description: Intermediate courses or seminars on topics beyond the scope of existing departmental offerings. See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: FYS: Dance & Movement Analysis
nodes.description: Intensive first-year seminar focused on specific themes and/or disciplinary perspectives. Emphasis on developing critical reading and writing skills, substantive revision, information literacy, and analytical thinking. First-year seminars are frequently organized to meet one of the disciplinary Catamount Core requirements. Topics vary by offering; periodic offering at intervals that may exceed four years.
nodes.other_requirements: CAS first time first year students only. HCOL students not eligible to enroll.

nodes.title: Ballet: Foundations
nodes.description: Introduction to applied practice in ballet. Open to students with no previous dance experience. Training in classical exercises and vocabulary, with focus on placement, coordination, basic anatomy, musicality, and movement quality. While primarily a studio course, video viewings with journal responses, live performance attendance, and group choreography assignments are required.

nodes.title: Teaching Assistantship
nodes.description: Undergraduate student service as a teaching assistant, usually in an introductory-level course in the discipline, for which credit is awarded. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Musical Theatre Dance
nodes.description: The art of dance in musical theatre with training in performance skills, vocabulary, choreography, and specific styles of musical theatre dance. Special emphasis on choreographers whose works influenced musical theatre dance. Reading, writing, and attending live performances required. Prerequisite: DNCE 1400.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor permission, PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Dance Performance Practicum
nodes.description: Participation in faculty-supervised dance performances; focus on rehearsal leading to fully realized public performances. Prerequisites: Audition or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Audition or Instructor permission. Time/location scheduled individually with students; 1-3 variable credits Participation in faculty-supervised dance performances; focus on rehearsal leading to fully realized public performance, Dancing Uphill. In coordination with the instructor and choreographers, students should be available, prepared, and present to dedicate significant time to studio practice, technical rehearsals (with lights, costumes, etc.), and fully produced final presentations.

nodes.title: Pilates and Yoga Workshop
nodes.description: Provides an experiential and intellectual exploration of Joseph Pilates's conditioning techniques and Hatha Yoga philosophy and practice, designed to develop lifelong physical fitness and mindful awareness. Students will learn safe and effective movement patterns using a progressive approach that emphasizes alignment, stability, concentration, and flow. Through a combination of Pilates matwork, Yoga asanas, breathwork, and meditation, participants will strengthen the body, calm the mind, and cultivate a deeper sense of balance and control.
nodes.other_requirements: Open to degree and PACE students

nodes.title: Honors
nodes.description: College honors thesis or other department/program honors, under the supervision of a faculty member. Offered at department discretion.

nodes.title: Dance Senior Capstone
nodes.description: A senior-level capstone course for dance majors, involving independent creative work/research in close consultation with a faculty sponsor on a specific and advanced project. Prerequisites: Nine hours of 2000-level DNCE courses; Senior standing; departmental permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Nine hours of 2000-level DNCE courses; Senior standing; departmental permission. Instructor permission required. Reserved for graduating dance majors. Contact instructor with questions.

nodes.title: COIL: Brazilian Dance
nodes.description: Exposure to Brazilian culture through embodied dance practice, informed by studies of music, race, ethnicity, and socioeconomic diversity in Brazil. Focus on Brazil's most popular and traditional dances. Reading, writing, and attending live performances required.

nodes.title: Jazz in American Dance
nodes.description: An in-depth study of the legacy and influence of African and African-derived dance forms on American social/vernacular dance, theatre and jazz dance, and hip hop. Prerequisites: A Catamount Core WIL1 course, DNCE 1500, DNCE 2600, three additional hours of Dance at the 2000-level or above; Dance majors or minors only; minimum Junior standing.
nodes.other_requirements: A Catamount Core WIL1 course; Dance majors or minors only; minimum Junior standing.

nodes.title: Choreography Workshop
nodes.description: Employing a variety of choreographic methodologies, students work toward developing their unique artistry in dance creation and performance through faculty-supervised projects. Special emphasis on creative collaboration with other artists and performance organization/marketing. Reading, writing, and attending live performances required. Prerequisites: DNCE 1200, DNCE 2600.
nodes.other_requirements: Contact pbesaw@uvm.edu if you have the prerequisite and are interested in enrolling in this course!

nodes.title: Contemporary: Intermediate
nodes.description: Intermediate level applied practice in contemporary dance. Emphasis on technical training, working toward expanded body awareness and strength, as well as compositional exploration. Reading, writing, and attending live performances required. May be repeated for credit. Prerequisites: DNCE 1100 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override.

nodes.title: Ballet: Intermediate
nodes.description: Intermediate level practice in ballet. Increased competence and stamina in the practice of classical vocabulary/exercises. Emphasis on expanded anatomical principles in dance, as well as developing expressive performance. Reading, writing, and attending live performances required. May be repeated for credit. Prerequisite: DNCE 1400 or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: PACE students with permission and override

nodes.title: Contemporary: Foundations
nodes.description: Introduction to applied practice in contemporary dance. Open to students with no previous dance training. Emphasis on fundamentals of contemporary dance technique and movement mechanics. Includes overview of modern/contemporary dance history and experiential anatomy. Reading, writing, and attending live performances required. May be repeated for credit.

nodes.title: Dance Repertory
nodes.description: Participation in the learning and rehearsal of dance choreography. May or may not be performed for the public. Prerequisite: Audition or Instructor permission.
nodes.other_requirements: Audition or Instructor permission. PACE students with permission and override.

nodes.title: Pilates & Yoga Workshop
nodes.description: Introductory courses or seminars on topics beyond the scope of existing departmental offerings. See Schedule of Courses for specific titles.

nodes.title: Dance Composition
nodes.description: A study of choreography, using craft and concept in the process of dance composition. Focus on developing original movement and ideas in the creation of choreographic studies/projects. Prerequisite: DNCE 1200.
nodes.other_requirements: Instructor's permission; DNCE majors or minors only; PACE students with permission and override. Students must be a dance major or minor and have taken DNCE 1200, or have instructor permission to take this class.
